channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok’s comment sections are being flooded with copy-pasted Christian messages
item.description: An influx of copy-and-pasted Christian messages has recently taken over TikTok’s comment sections. Over the past several days, comments about Jesus Christ have surfaced among the top comments on a wide range of unrelated videos.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: A new wearable from dating app RAW promises to track your partner’s emotions in real time
item.description: Forget a diamond ring, the latest symbol of commitment now comes in the form of wearable tech. The RAW ring, created by the dating app RAW and Queens Tech, allows couples to track each other’s emotions, both good and bad.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Laser-wielding robots are redefining farming
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Aptori Now on Google Cloud Marketplace for AI-Powered Security and Automated Risk Remediation
item.description: Aptori’s AI-Driven AppSec Platform Proactively Eliminates Vulnerabilities to Minimize Risk and Ensure Compliance.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump floats labeling violence against Tesla dealerships as domestic terrorism
item.description: President Trump hinted he would be open to labeling individuals who carry out violence at Tesla dealerships as “domestic terrorists” following a string of violent demonstrations at the electric vehicle company’s showrooms across the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Great Lakes Shipwreck Historical Society Uncovers Wreckage of 1892 Cargo Ship
item.description: Researchers from the Great Lakes Shipwreck Historical Society have discovered the wreckage of a 300-foot cargo ship that sank in Lake Superior in 1892.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: People are paying up for this $39 yogurt that went viral on TikTok
item.description: We’re only in the third month of the year and already there have been a number of bizarre food trends go viral on TikTok—from a $19 strawberry to feeding babies spoonfuls of butter. The latest is a yogurt, called Coconut Cult, that costs $39 for a 16 ounce jar.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works. Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the United States.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Two-thirds of Silicon Valley tech workers are foreign-born, new report reveals
item.description: Of tech workers with a bachelor's degree or higher, 23% came from India and 18% from China, while 17% were born in California, according to the Silicon Valley Index.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Albania shuts down TikTok for a year amid concerns over violence among children
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — The Albanian Cabinet decided on Thursday to shut down TikTok for 12 months, blaming the popular video-sharing platform for inciting violence and bullying, especially among children.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: HPE cuts 2,500 jobs, remains committed to Juniper buy, faces tariff issues
item.description: After sharing a mostly positive revenue report for the first quarter of its 2025 fiscal year, HPE executives detailed a number of challenges the company will face in the coming months, including layoffs, a court case over its proposed buy of Juniper Networks, and the U.S. government’s tariff plan.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Siemens announces $285 million investment in US manufacturing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Siemens plans to invest $285 million in manufacturing in the United States, the tech company said Thursday, including two new facilities in California and Texas. The U.S. is the largest market for the company. The recent investments in the Siemens' U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, ABC 4
